figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of existing multi-temporal remote sensing datasets for change perception and understanding.

Table 3: Change QA performance of VLMs on Delta-SECOND. Metrics include Accuracy for choice questions (CIC), andMETEOR (M) / CIDEr (C) for open-ended questions (CQS, CTI, CSA). * denotes fine-tuned baselines.

Table 2: Semantic change detection (SCD) results on Delta-SECOND. Best scores for specialized and reasoning models are shaded separately. Abbreviations: L. Veg. (Low Vegetation), Grnd. (Ground), Build. (Building), and Play. (Playground). * denotes fine-tuned baselines.
Figure 12: Qualitative comparison of Delta-LLaVA and other models for bi-temporal change understanding.
Figure 2: An overview of the data annotation framework for the Delta-QA dataset.

Figure 8: Overall architecture of Delta-LLaVA. The VCP module explicitly amplifies differences in imagery feature. Visual, Change, and Prompt tokens generated by Change-SEG are concatenated with text tokens and fed into the LLM to jointly output textual answers and segmentation masks.
